title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxbackup argument is set to 10 or as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxbackup argument is set to 10 or as appropriate
input_row.description: Retain 10 or an appropriate number of old log files.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes automatically rotates the log files. Retaining old log files ensures that you would have sufficient log data available for carrying out any investigation or correlation. For example, if you have set file size of 100 MB and the number of old log files to keep as 10, you would approximate have 1 GB of log data that you could potentially use for your analysis.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --audit-log-maxbackup argument is set to 10 or as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and set --audit-log-maxbackup to 10 or as appropriate. kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment -- namespace=federation-system Impact: None Default Value: By default, auditing is not enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/federation-apiserver  207 | P a g e 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/federation/manifests/fe deration-apiserver-deployment.yaml 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/
input_row.impact: None Default Value: By default, auditing is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_audit_log_maxbackup_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Enumerate all Deployments (or Pods, if not managed by a Deployment) in the relevant namespace (commonly federation-system) to identify the federation-apiserver.
- Use the Kubernetes API or kubectl to retrieve the spec of the federation-apiserver Deployment:
  - Command: kubectl -n federation-system get deployment federation-apiserver-deployment -o json
- Inspect the container spec(s) for the federation-apiserver and parse the command/args array.
- Search for the presence of the --audit-log-maxbackup flag.
- Validate that the value for --audit-log-maxbackup is set to 10 or a value appropriate to organizational policy (default secure value is 10 as per CIS).
- Pass Criteria: The --audit-log-maxbackup argument is present and set to 10 or greater (or as per documented policy).
- Fail Criteria: The --audit-log-maxbackup argument is missing, or set to less than 10, or not set according to policy.
- Example check (pseudo-code):
  - For each container in the federation-apiserver deployment:
    - If "--audit-log-maxbackup" flag exists and value >= 10 → PASS
    - Else → FAIL

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
